analysis.reasoning: Core message: A warning that any attack by Iran on the United States will trigger a U.S. response 1,000 times greater. This is a deterrent threat portraying Iran as an aggressor and the U.S. as willing to retaliate massively. Evaluation by narrative: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not promoted; no reference to values, history, or tradition. US2 (admirable society) – Not relevant; no mention of society, diversity, or heroes. US3 (hostile enemies) – Promoted; it frames Iran as an adversary threatening the U.S., justifying deterrence. US4 (force for good) – Not promoted; no reference to human rights or benevolent actions. US5 (great international ally) – Not mentioned; no allies referenced. US6 (at the forefront) – Arguably touches on military strength, but the tweet is a threat rather than positioning broad leadership; insufficient to claim leadership framing. Conclusion: The tweet promotes US3.
